title: Terrible redesign
text: The 2025-26 redesign is an unusable mess. It depends heavily on location and has poor real-time access while in the subway, which was better with the previous version. You lose a lot of the system map, while you’re traveling in system, making it harder to anticipate upcoming stops on unfamiliar lines. Findability is really poor. It’s also difficult to find line-specific maps. If a long-time New Yorker struggles with it, I don’t know how a tourist would succeed in using this version.

text: I can only assume that the people giving this redesign  positive reviews are using it mostly for the train, which - fine. But the way the bus information is organized is an absolute disaster. When I search for a bus route, it pulls up every single bus stop on the route regardless of where in space I am. Why would it not pull up the closest stop to my current location on that line? It also won't let you choose which direction you're going in while searching, so it'll just show you, again, every available stop and force you to either scroll or type in the entire stop, which is annoying when you don't know the stop by heart.   PLEASE work on the UX here. This is still the only app with somewhat accurate arrival times, so I am forced to use it, but god is it a miserable experience.

title: Looks good I guess?
text: The most recent major update does look slick but it's much less usable, a trend I'm not excited about at all.   My favorite thing about the app was that you open the home screen and you have your pinned trips and the subway map easily accessible.   Now you can only favorite a specific line at a station, and it will always default to showing only uptown trains for that line (regardless of whether that train actually has uptown trips from that station). Every time I need to see this info I have to open the app, swipe up to expand the trip info, and switch direction.   Station selection is also a huge pain now, meaning that you effectively *have* to have location access on unless you want to spend 5 minutes every time you want to check a station you don't frequently visit.   The maps have been completely hidden for some incomprehensible reason, now instead of a maps button on the GPS map you have to go to More -> subway maps.   None of this makes the app unusable but it makes it far less convenient, and when you're trying to frantically look up routes in the middle of a subway station convenience is at a premium. I will probably just download a pdf of the subway map and use google maps for times, because it's now just the simpler method.

title: new interface is unusable if you’re trying to plan in advance (2 stars for accuracy)
text: now it only shows you upcoming train times in your exact location, and you have to dig to view a specific station in that same transit line. that sounds nice on paper, but it’s really impractical and doesn’t allow for critical advance planning. many times i’m walking to a station further away, and i wanna know what time i’ll need to get there. other times i’m on a train with multiple viable transfers and i need to see which future transfer is coming the quickest, so that i know where to hop off. i ride on the E to 145, which means i can either get off at 7ave and take the D or i can get off at 42nd and take the A, that’s not information that you’ll receive if it’s only showing you nearby train stops.   favorites don’t save this either. for one, you run into the same issue, it’ll only show you the station nearest you, even within your favorites. but there’s another relating issue. i have multiple “favorite” locations, so i previously used the favorites tab often, but it’s a total mess. because it’s always trying to push you to train times near your location, the amount of text space that information takes up is a total waste of visual space for me and a one page list has turned into a constant scroll to find the train line i wanted to see.   the main issues (to me) with the old system was that it was buggy, and that you had a few too many tap throughs on the buses page to switch directions. now they’ve just added wayyy too many things to tap through to get to a basic piece of information.   this was my go-to app because it was the most reliable for exact times, but i’d rather an app be slightly off with an arrival time than be a waste of my time. it used to be simple and fast enough (when it wasn’t buggy) for me to check on future transfers while i was underground in-between stops, but i’d never be able to pull that with the redesign. i tried it for a few days and gave it a proper shot but i deleted it.   oh, and i almost forgot! they got rid of the widget! why???? that was so useful for getting quick information, and i could tap through to get more info on that specific train line. thankfully i found a better app that has it for free, but still. that alone made me want to delete the app.   new yorkers are fast and we need a fast app. keep it plain, simple and efficient

text: This was my go to app for transit (checking train times and bus location on the map mostly), and this new update has made it much harder for me to get the info I need. I used to be able to search for a bus line and see every active bus on the map in both directions for that bus. I was also able to look up a subway line and see every stop easily for that line. With the new update, i can only search based on the bus or subway stop. I don't always know the bus stop, because even though I may know the bus's general route, I may not know the exact cross-streets of the bus stop closest to me and it requires extra investigation to figure that out. The app is now only useful for searching for a bus or train stop on a line I am very familiar with. Also, when I see the bus map, it doesn't even show the bus stops on the map anymore, which is pretty silly to me. It would greatly benefit me if the search functionality was the way it was before this last update. In general, I think it should line-based and less stop-based. Thank you.

title: Bring back the old app
text: As a native New Yorker who has seen several different regime changes at the MTA, one thing remains constant - the bewildering desire to make things harder than they have to be. From the new multimillion dollar turnstiles, to OMNY, to this app…WHY?   Requiring location services to be enabled for app usage is invasive and unnecessary. Most of us aren’t ok with big brother tracking our every move and selling our data to continue fueling this decaying capitalist hell hole. We were also perfectly fine with the previous app’s ability to let us click into a subway line and see the entire station list and corresponding train times. This new version isn’t intuitive and forces the selection of “favorites” (again, unnecessarily) in order to get any quick insight into what’s happening at a particular station. As New Yorkers on the go, our trips can literally start anywhere in the city! We’re not always at home. Please bring back the previous app’s more streamlined navigation. I’m all for enhancements, but only if they’re actually going to…well…enhance.

title: Absolutely Terrible Update
text: I don’t know what compelled the MTA to release this update, but whoever was responsible for this should be fired immediately. This update is horrendous and should never have seen the light of day. If the goal was to disrupt, anger, and inconvenience the thousands of New Yorkers who relied on this app for travel, then congratulations because you’ve succeeded in doing just that. This is only time I can think of in recent memory where an update has less practical functionality than the previous version. Opening the app to a tab of all my favorited train stations and bus stops? Gone. An easily navigable and simple user interface? Gone. The only two things I appreciate in this update is the swap direction button for the subways and the layout of the subway tab in the status page, but at the expense of everything else that made this app great to use, I’d rather have the old version back. This is what happens when you design apps for the approval of your corporate stakeholders instead of the people who use the app everyday. Do better next time. Edit: The times for the trains and buses aren’t even accurate anymore. I nearly missed my train multiple times because the app would say 5 minutes but the train was already approaching the station. title: Unreliable Bus Schedule a Haiku
text: Check MTA app, Bus in 5 minutes, then POOF! No driver assigned.  I have learned to distrust the schedule listed on the MTA app. The buses almost never depart at their scheduled times. This is especially true with the M104.  When I mention it to the Chat agent in the MTA app, they say it’s due to lack of a driver being assigned to a route. This is news to me. Why bother keeping that time on the schedule?  If it’s labor shortages, fine but make the adjustments. Remove it from the schedule or add an additional status, “No driver assigned.”  The other issue is buses disappearing from the map. It’s approaching my stop then POOF! It’s not longer there. Instead, it’s a bus that is Not In Service. Can drivers flip their trackers off? If so, then why bother even listing buses on the app. Just go back to the printed timetables.
